key_insight: A symmetry-aware generative model can design flat-band materials from unconventional crystal skeletons beyond the usual kagome/Lieb/pyrochlore motif catalog.
engineering_relevance: This expands the searchable design space for quantum materials discovery by generating symmetry-compatible, potentially synthesizable candidates with flat electronic bands.
method: Represent sublattice geometry continuously to explore nonstandard crystal motifs
Use SkeleGen to place novel skeletons on symmetry-compatible Wyckoff positions
Denoise surrounding chemistry and screen candidates for stability and flat-band behavior
evidence_signal: The workflow produced 9,352 crystal candidates that passed stability and flatness screening, and high-throughput DFT confirmed flat bands consistent with tight-binding spectra of the isolated skeletons.
watch_item: Flat-band predictions depend on geometric connectivity and DFT screening, so experimental synthesizability and robustness to distortions/disorder still need validation.
paper_title: Symmetry-aware generative design of flat-band materials beyond known crystal-net prototypes
